# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  DOCKER_DRIVER: overlay2d
  
gitlab-ci:
  stage: test
  image_name: docker:latest
  services:
    - name: registry/docker
      alias: docker
      
  before_script:
     # Pull the latest version of Dockerfile from GitLab repository.
     - 'which ssh-agent || ( apt-get update && apt-get install openssh-client )'  
     - eval $(ssh-agent -s)
     - echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" | tr -d '\r\n' | ssh-add - > /dev/null 2>&1   
      # Pull the latest version of Dockerfile from GitLab repository.  
     - curl https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/$CI_PROJECT_ID/mirror/master/.git/config --output .git-mirror 2> /dev/null || ( mkdir .git && echo "http://source.example.org ${GITLAB_URL}/api/v4/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}" > .git/config )
     - git fetch --unshallow master   # Fetch the latest code from GitLab repository, including all submodules and tags 
     
  script:   
       echo "Running tests..." && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Tests passed"  && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Building Docker image..." && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Docker image built" && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Pushing Docker image to registry..." && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Docker image pushed" && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Running integration tests..." && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Integration tests passed" && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "All tests passed" && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Building release notes..." && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
      - echo "Release notes built" && sleep 10 & wait $$LINODEMITTERID$$; exit $? ;# replace with your test command. For example 'npm run build' or './gradlew clean build'.   # Replace the following line by a real one that fits in here and uncomment it
